doc_title: A Neural Network Architecture Combining Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) and Support Vector Machine (SVM) for Intrusion Detection in Network Traffic Data

items.evidence_span: Google TensorFlow[1] was used to implement the neural network models in this study
Similar to the work of Alalshekmubarak \& Smith (2013)[2] and Tang (2013)[22], the present paper proposes to use SVM as the classifier in a neural network architecture.
Binning reduces the required computational cost, and improves the classification performance on the dataset[15].
This process was done using the qcut () function of pandas[16].
the StandardScaler().fit_transform() function of Scikit-learn[19] was used for the data standardization in this study.

It has 24 statistical features[20]; (1) 14 features from the KDD Cup 1999 dataset[21], and (2) 10 additional features, which according to Song, Takakura, \& Okabe (2006)[20], might be pivotal in a more effective investigation on intrusion detection.

It has 24 statistical features[20]; (1) 14 features from the KDD Cup 1999 dataset[21], and (2) 10 additional features, which according to Song, Takakura, \& Okabe (2006)[20], might be pivotal in a more effective investigation on intrusion detection.
Similar to the work of Alalshekmubarak \& Smith (2013)[2] and Tang (2013)[22], the present paper proposes to use SVM as the classifier in a neural network architecture.
